title: Goroutines, Channels, Select, Ownership, and Pipelines
description: A report pipeline must process bounded work concurrently, preserve result ownership, close channels correctly, and terminate every goroutine. Learners cumulatively produce a bounded concurrent report pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Start goroutines only with named ownership, inputs, outputs, stop conditions, panic boundary, and completion evidence.
Use unbuffered, buffered, and directional channels while assigning close authority to the sender that knows no values remain.
Distinguish open, closed, empty, nil, and buffered channel states and interpret two-result receives and range termination.
